title: Gendo Ikari the Japanese Zero Pilot
display_title: Gendo Ikari the Japanese Zero Pilot
wikitext:
[]: Gendo Ikari the Japanese Zero Pilot             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Jul 23, 2008 2:12 am Ill try find out some more about this guy. [URL=http://img234.imageshack.us/my.php?image=picxt6.jpg] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jul 23, 2008 5:40 am That's cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 23, 2008 11:05 am Nice that you were able to get pictures! Interesting stuff!

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Jul 23, 2008 11:07 am Neat.

[]:             Quote Post by Admetus » Wed Jul 23, 2008 12:46 pm Looked up the 'zero' on wiki and it can be refered to as a Rei-sen. And they called their models A6M1 Type 0 etc. Just like Eva Type 00. Interesting indeed.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Wed Jul 23, 2008 12:56 pm No, zero was a fighter aircraft that Japan used during the war (IIRC). Know your history.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jul 23, 2008 1:02 pm this is rather fascinating I haven't heard of this before though, so I wonder if it's just a coincidence or this was direct inspiration where did you find this Zeak?

[]:             Quote Post by Admetus » Wed Jul 23, 2008 1:05 pm Huh? Who said that the 'zero' was not a fighter aircraft? lol

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Wed Jul 23, 2008 1:11 pm ^ Huh? Oh, my mistake. I thought that was what you ment.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jul 24, 2008 1:06 pm Well, it certainly gives "Any resemblance to any persons, living or dead, is strictly coincidental" a run for its money...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jul 24, 2008 3:46 pm What's the deal? I don't see anything connecting between them other than name

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Thu Jul 24, 2008 4:01 pm Lol. Its just irony, man. No one has suggested the character was named after this guy. Just ironic, thats all.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Jul 24, 2008 4:25 pm Xard wrote: I don't see anything connecting between them other than name

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Jul 24, 2008 4:54 pm Zuggy wrote: Xard wrote: I don't see anything connecting between them other than name

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 24, 2008 6:42 pm Rei can mean zero or soul. Rei's the pilot of Unit zero and it's soul is most likely Rei 1. Does that help make more sense?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Jul 24, 2008 8:29 pm In what language is that referring to, that was what I was looking for. I got the connection the first time, but I didn't know what it meant on a language level.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 24, 2008 8:49 pm Whoops, I knew I was forgetting something. My example of Rei is Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Jul 24, 2008 8:51 pm I thought so, it all comes together to me now. Maybe there is some kind of connection between this and the show that Anno never let out...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 24, 2008 9:04 pm It would be so awesome if it turned out he had some kind of connection with Konrad Lorenz.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Jul 24, 2008 10:11 pm wat, Gendo lost his Rei....
summary:
[]: Gendo Ikari the Japanese Zero Pilot             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Jul 23, 2008 2:12 am Ill try find out some more about this guy. [URL=http://img234.imageshack.us/my.php?image=picxt6.jpg] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jul 23, 2008 5:40 am That's cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust &ra
